rulings.Case Title: REGINA PACHECO VS. MINDFUL VENTURE CAPITAL MANAGEMENT, LLC, ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Extending Examination Lien Pursuant To Ccp 708.110.
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion/Discovery Calendar for Friday, May 30, 2025, line 1, PLAINTIFF REGINA PACHECO AN INDIVIDUAL Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Extending Examination Lien Pursuant To Ccp 708.110. Off calendar. Parties to contact Department 505 to re-set this motion. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. = (302/JMQ))

rulings.Case Title: JONATHAN MOORE VS. BYER CALIFORNIA, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Preliminary Approval Of Class Settlement
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion/Discovery Calendar for Friday, May 30, 2025, line 13, PLAINTIFF JONATHAN MOORE, AN INDIVIDUAL, ON BEHALF OF HIMSELF AND ON BEHALF OF ALL PERSONS SIMILARLY SITUATED Motion For Preliminary Approval Of Class Settlement Plaintiff Jonathan Moore's unopposed Motion For Preliminary Approval Of Class Settlement is GRANTED. Good cause appearing, the court: (1) preliminarily approves the proposed settlement of this class action with Defendant Byer California ("Defendant"); (2) for settlement purposes only, conditionally certifies the Class, which is comprised of "all individuals who were employed by Defendant in California and classified as non-exempt employees at any time during the Class Period", which is April 17, 2019 to October 14, 2024; (3) provisionally appoints Plaintiff as the representatives of the Class; (4) provisionally appoints Norman B. Blumenthal, Kyle R. Nordrehaug, and Aparajit Bhowmik of Blumenthal Nordrehaug Bhowmik De Blouw LLP as Class Counsel; (5) approves the form and method for providing class-wide notice; (6) directing that notice of the proposed settlement be given to the class; (7) appointing ILYM Group, Inc. as Administrator, and (8) sets a final approval hearing date for September 25, 2025 at 9:00 am in this department, for which Plaintiff shall file and serve a memorandum identifying and discussing issues that may be discussed no later than 10 court days before the hearing, with courtesy copy delivered to the department within 24 hours of filing. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. = (302/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: SENSORTOWER, INC. VS. TRILLER, INC., ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Setting Aside Dismissal And For Entry Of Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion/Discovery Calendar for Friday, May 30, 2025, line 2, PLAINTIFF SENSORTOWER, INC.'S Motion For Order Setting Aside Dismissal And For Entry Of Judgment Plaintiff Sensortower, Inc.'s Motion For Order Setting Aside Dismissal And For Entry Of Judgment is GRANTED IN PART. Good cause appearing, the court sets aside the dismissal without prejudice previously filed in this case and authorizes entry of judgment in favor of Plaintiff and against Defendant Triller, Inc. in the amount of $541,020. The court cannot enter judgment as requested against Twiller Platform Co. This was not the party named in the suit and it is not a party to the Stipulation for Entry of Judgment. Plaintiff is admonished that it is not free on its own to change the caption. A change to the caption, including a change of defendant, requires a court order. All pleadings and papers filed in this action must be filed using the appropriate caption. Plaintiff will have to follow the Code regarding submission of a proposed judgment. Going forward, best practice is to submit the proposed judgment with the moving papers. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for Plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. = (302/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: FRANK M. VILCHES VS. GRACE B. VILCHEZ ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiff'S Special Interrogatories, Set One, And Plaintiff'S Form Interrogatories, Set One, Propounded On Defendant Jeanette Vilches
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion/Discovery Calendar for Friday, May 30, 2025, line 11, PLAINTIFF FRANK VILCHES'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiff'S Special Interrogatories, Set One, And Plaintiff'S Form Interrogatories, Set One, Propounded On Defendant Jeanette Vilches Plaintiff Frank M. Vilches's motion to compel further responses from Defendant Jeanette Vilches to special interrogatories (set one) and form interrogatories (set one) and monetary sanctions is GRANTED. The interrogatories at issue are reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence. (See Code of Civil Procedure section 2017.010.) Plaintiff has shown that Defendant Jeanette Vilches' responses are incomplete and none of the objections has merit. Indeed, Defendant's discovery responses do not raise the "no good cause" objection that the opposition now relies upon. The compound objection lacked merit since it is only problematic when the inquiry pertains to numerous subjects (see Clement v. Alegre (2009) 177 Cal.App.4th 1277, 1291) and Plaintiff told Defendant that she could break her answers into parts if she wished. Defendant did not even respond to some of the interrogatories. (See Special Interrogatory 11.) Plaintiff demonstrates a sufficient meet and confer. Within 10 days of notice of entry of this order, defendant shall provide further responses to special interrogatories 5, 6, 8, and 11-16 and form interrogatories 2.5 and 15.1. Defendant Jeanette Vilches did not act with substantial justification and shall pay Plaintiff $2,725 as sanctions within 14 days of notice of entry of this order. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. = (302/JMQ)
